title: urera mannii
intro: Urera mannii, also known as the Stinging Nettle Tree or Wild Nettle, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family Urticaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, Madagascar, and the Mascarene Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Urera mannii is often prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, fever, and digestive problems.
